_comment: AI surface inventory (NF-AIOPS-004): every component that calls an LLM, generates operator-visible text, can recommend actions, or can execute actions - and its policy-coverage status. The objective is one safety boundary, one policy engine, one audit trail; this file is the ledger of where that boundary stands. tests/test_units.py cross-checks the netframe-monitor rows against the SOURCE, so a row here cannot claim coverage the code does not have; rows in other repos are verified by the listed method and date. UNGATED rows are defects by definition.

surfaces.enforcement_point: enforce(source=interpreter) before write_report; web page renders the screened file
enforce(source=console) in answer(); action TAGS additionally sanitised by _guard_actions
surfaces.evidence_coverage: not-yet (console answers are conversational, not material-finding reports; candidate follow-up)

surfaces.enforcement_point: enforce(source=chief); highest-authority report (72B, read as executive prioritisation)
enforce(source=predict) on the narration only; deterministic tables bypass the LLM entirely
surfaces.verified_by: live block observed (2x POL-002 on first gated run)

surfaces.enforcement_point: delegates to netframe_chat.answer(), which is gated; serves only screened output
surfaces.verified_by: source review: 3 fixed routes, no generation

surfaces.enforcement_point: probes the console over HTTP; consumes gated output, generates nothing
Tier-1 allowlist + explicit approve + >=95% confidence gate + audit ledger; the ONLY execution path in this repo
renders only report files that were screened at write time; generates nothing itself

policy_bridge.screen_llm() on both model-prose exits (imports the shared engine, no rule copies); execution via per-node allowlist + explicit Discord confirm; mutation lifecycle mirrored to the hash-chained ledger with approver identity (oncall_* events)
surfaces.verified_by: live bridge test on Jarvis: block + ledger + chain verify

surfaces.enforcement_point: NONE - a general-purpose OpenAI-compatible model API. Its consumers (Open WebUI chat) are conversational, not operational telemetry with Jarvis's authority behind it. Documented as a deliberate boundary decision, NOT an oversight: gating raw chat would block ordinary conversation about e.g. disks. Revisit if it ever gains operational context or execution.
surfaces.verified_by: boundary decision recorded in NF-AIOPS-004 follow-up

surfaces.enforcement_point: NONE, by the same recorded decision as llm_router (Open WebUI is its front-end): general-purpose conversational chat with no operational telemetry, no Jarvis authority behind its answers, and no execution path. Gating it would block ordinary conversation. Revisit if it ever gains tool-calling into the estate.

_evidence_engine: netframe-monitor/netframe_evidence.py (score + section_for_current_state); SINGLE shared implementation - no path computes its own quality/confidence or renders a variant
